Currently, skill generation is not only label-intensive due to manual authoring, but also may suffer from human--machine cognitive misalignment, which can lead to degraded agent performance, as evidenced by evaluations on SkillsBench. Therefore, we aim to enable agents to autonomously generate skills. However, existing self-evolving methods designed for tools cannot be directly applied to skills due to their increased complexity. To address these issues, we propose EvoSkills, a self-evolving skills framework that enables agents to autonomously construct complex, multi-file skill packages. Specifically, EvoSkills couples a Skill Generator that iteratively refines skills with a Surrogate Verifier that co-evolves to provide informative and actionable feedback without access to ground-truth test content. On SkillsBench, EvoSkills achieves the highest pass rate among five baselines on both Claude Code and Codex, and also exhibits strong generalization capabilities to six additional LLMs.
title_zh: EvoSkills：通过共同进化验证实现自我演化的智能体技能
abstract_zh: Anthropic 提出了“技能”（skills）这一概念，旨在让大型语言模型（LLM）代理能够应对那些仅靠简单工具调用无法解决的多步骤专业任务。其中，工具是单一且自包含的函数，而技能则是一组结构化的、相互依赖的多文件资源包。然而，当前技能的生成不仅因需要人工编写而具有高度标签依赖性，还可能因人机认知不匹配而导致代理性能下降，这一点已在 SkillsBench 的评估中得到验证。因此，我们致力于使代理能够自主生成技能。然而，现有的针对工具的自演化方法由于技能本身的复杂性，无法直接应用于技能生成。为解决这些问题，我们提出了 EvoSkills——一种自演化技能框架，使代理能够自主构建复杂的、多文件的技能包。具体而言，EvoSkills 结合了一个技能生成器（Skill Generator），它通过迭代方式不断优化技能，以及一个代理验证器（Surrogate Verifier），该验证器在不访问真实测试内容的情况下协同演化，提供富有信息量且可操作的反馈。在 SkillsBench 上，EvoSkills 在 Claude Code 和 Codex 两种模型上均取得了五种基线方法中的最高通过率，并展现出对六种额外 LLM 的强大泛化能力。

title: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
authors: Ao Qu, Han Zheng, Zijian Zhou, Yihao Yan, Yihong Tang, Shao Yong Ong, Fenglu Hong, Kaichen Zhou, Chonghe Jiang, Minwei Kong, Jiacheng Zhu, Xuan Jiang, Sirui Li, Cathy Wu, Bryan Kian Hsiang Low, Jinhua Zhao, Paul Pu Liang
abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based evolution is a promising approach for open-ended discovery, where progress requires sustained search and knowledge accumulation. Existing methods still rely heavily on fixed heuristics and hard-coded exploration rules, which limit the autonomy of LLM agents. We present CORAL, the first framework for autonomous multi-agent evolution on open-ended problems. CORAL replaces rigid control with long-running agents that explore, reflect, and collaborate through shared persistent memory, asynchronous multi-agent execution, and heartbeat-based interventions. It also provides practical safeguards, including isolated workspaces, evaluator separation, resource management, and agent session and health management. Evaluated on diverse mathematical, algorithmic, and systems optimization tasks, CORAL sets new state-of-the-art results on 10 tasks, achieving 3-10 times higher improvement rates with far fewer evaluations than fixed evolutionary search baselines across tasks. On Anthropic's kernel engineering task, four co-evolving agents improve the best known score from 1363 to 1103 cycles. Mechanistic analyses further show how these gains arise from knowledge reuse and multi-agent exploration and communication. Together, these results suggest that greater agent autonomy and multi-agent evolution can substantially improve open-ended discovery. Code is available at https://github.com/Human-Agent-Society/CORAL.
title_zh: 珊瑚：面向开放式发现的自主多智能体演化
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的演化是一种极具前景的开放式发现方法，其进展依赖于持续的探索与知识积累。然而，现有方法仍严重依赖固定的启发式规则和硬编码的探索策略，限制了LLM智能体的自主性。我们提出了CORAL——首个面向开放式问题的自主多智能体演化框架。CORAL通过长期运行的智能体，借助共享的持久化记忆、异步多智能体执行以及基于心跳机制的干预，取代了僵化的控制方式，实现智能体的自主探索、反思与协作。同时，该框架还提供了实用的安全保障措施，包括隔离的工作空间、评估器分离、资源管理以及对智能体会话和健康状态的管理。在多种数学、算法及系统优化任务上的评估表明，CORAL在10项任务上达到了新的最先进水平，在各项任务中以远少于固定演化搜索基线的评估次数，实现了3至10倍更高的改进率。在Anthropic的内核工程任务中，四个协同进化的智能体将最佳已知得分从1363周期提升至1103周期。机制分析进一步揭示了这些性能提升源于知识复用以及多智能体间的探索与通信。综合来看，这些结果表明，更高的智能体自主性与多智能体演化能够显著提升开放式发现的能力。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/Human-Agent-Society/CORAL。

title: GraphWalk: Enabling Reasoning in Large Language Models through Tool-Based Graph Navigation
authors: Taraneh Ghandi, Hamidreza Mahyar, Shachar Klaiman
abstract: The use of knowledge graphs for grounding agents in real-world Q&A applications has become increasingly common. Answering complex queries often requires multi-hop reasoning and the ability to navigate vast relational structures. Standard approaches rely on prompting techniques that steer large language models to reason over raw graph context, or retrieval-augmented generation pipelines where relevant subgraphs are injected into the context. These, however, face severe limitations with enterprise-scale KGs that cannot fit in even the largest context windows available today. We present GraphWalk, a problem-agnostic, training-free, tool-based framework that allows off-the-shelf LLMs to reason through sequential graph navigation, dramatically increasing performance across different tasks. Unlike task-specific agent frameworks that encode domain knowledge into specialized tools, GraphWalk equips the LLM with a minimal set of orthogonal graph operations sufficient to traverse any graph structure. We evaluate whether models equipped with GraphWalk can compose these operations into correct multi-step reasoning chains, where each tool call represents a verifiable step creating a transparent execution trace. We first demonstrate our approach on maze traversal, a problem non-reasoning models are completely unable to solve, then present results on graphs resembling real-world enterprise knowledge graphs. To isolate structural reasoning from world knowledge, we evaluate on entirely synthetic graphs with random, non-semantic labels. Our benchmark spans 12 query templates from basic retrieval to compound first-order logic queries. Results show that tool-based traversal yields substantial and consistent gains over in-context baselines across all model families tested, with gains becoming more pronounced as scale increases, precisely where in-context approaches fail catastrophically.
title_zh: GraphWalk：通过基于工具的图导航实现大语言模型中的推理
abstract_zh: 将知识图谱用于在现实世界问答应用中为智能体提供语义基础，正变得越来越普遍。回答复杂查询通常需要多跳推理能力以及在庞大关系结构中导航的能力。目前的标准方法依赖于提示工程技术，引导大型语言模型对原始图谱上下文进行推理，或采用检索增强生成（RAG）流程，将相关子图注入上下文。然而，这些方法在企业级知识图谱面前面临严重局限——这类图谱规模巨大，甚至无法容纳在当今最大的上下文窗口中。我们提出 GraphWalk，一种与具体问题无关、无需训练、基于工具的框架，使现成的大型语言模型能够通过顺序图遍历进行推理，从而在多种任务上显著提升性能。与将领域知识编码进专用工具的任务特定代理框架不同，GraphWalk 仅向语言模型赋予一组最小且相互正交的图操作，足以遍历任意图结构。我们评估了配备 GraphWalk 的模型是否能将这些操作组合成正确的多步推理链，其中每一步工具调用都代表一个可验证的步骤，形成透明的执行轨迹。我们首先在迷宫遍历问题上展示该方法的有效性——这是非推理型模型完全无法解决的问题；随后展示了在模拟真实企业级知识图谱的图结构上的实验结果。为隔离结构化推理与世界知识的影响，我们在完全合成的图上进行评估，其节点标签随机且无语义。我们的基准测试涵盖从基础检索到复合一阶逻辑查询在内的12种查询模板。结果表明，基于工具的遍历方法在所有测试模型族中均显著且一致地优于上下文内基线方法，且随着模型规模增大，优势愈发明显——这正是上下文内方法灾难性失效的区域。

title: ByteRover: Agent-Native Memory Through LLM-Curated Hierarchical Context
authors: Andy Nguyen, Danh Doan, Hoang Pham, Bao Ha, Dat Pham, Linh Nguyen, Hieu Nguyen, Thien Nguyen, Cuong Do, Phat Nguyen, Toan Nguyen
abstract: Memory-Augmented Generation (MAG) extends large language models with external memory to support long-context reasoning, but existing approaches universally treat memory as an external service that agents call into, delegating storage to separate pipelines of chunking, embedding, and graph extraction. This architectural separation means the system that stores knowledge does not understand it, leading to semantic drift between what the agent intended to remember and what the pipeline actually captured, loss of coordination context across agents, and fragile recovery after failures. In this paper, we propose ByteRover, an agent-native memory architecture that inverts the memory pipeline: the same LLM that reasons about a task also curates, structures, and retrieves knowledge. ByteRover represents knowledge in a hierarchical Context Tree, a file-based knowledge graph organized as Domain, Topic, Subtopic, and Entry, where each entry carries explicit relations, provenance, and an Adaptive Knowledge Lifecycle (AKL) with importance scoring, maturity tiers, and recency decay. Retrieval uses a 5-tier progressive strategy that resolves most queries at sub-100 ms latency without LLM calls, escalating to agentic reasoning only for novel questions. Experiments on LoCoMo and LongMemEval demonstrate that ByteRover achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on LoCoMo and competitive results on LongMemEval while requiring zero external infrastructure, no vector database, no graph database, no embedding service, with all knowledge stored as human-readable markdown files on the local filesystem.
title_zh: ByteRover：通过LLM精选的分层上下文实现原生代理记忆
abstract_zh:
记忆增强生成（Memory-Augmented Generation, MAG）通过引入外部记忆来扩展大型语言模型，以支持长上下文推理。然而，现有方法普遍将记忆视为一种外部服务，由智能体调用，将存储任务交由独立的分块、嵌入和图谱提取流水线处理。这种架构上的分离导致知识存储系统无法真正理解所存储的内容，从而引发智能体意图记忆的信息与实际捕获内容之间的语义漂移，跨智能体协作时协调上下文的丢失，以及在故障后难以恢复的问题。

本文提出ByteRover——一种原生面向智能体的记忆架构，其核心思想是反转传统的记忆处理流程：负责任务推理的同一LLM同时承担知识的整理、组织与检索工作。ByteRover采用分层的“上下文树”（Context Tree）结构来表示知识，这是一种基于文件的知识图谱，按领域（Domain）、主题（Topic）、子主题（Subtopic）和条目（Entry）逐级组织，每个条目均包含明确的关系信息、来源记录，以及具备自适应知识生命周期（Adaptive Knowledge Lifecycle, AKL）机制，包括重要性评分、成熟度等级和时效衰减策略。

在检索方面，ByteRover采用五级渐进式策略，绝大多数查询可在100毫秒以内完成，且无需调用LLM；仅当面对全新问题时，才升级至代理式推理。在LoCoMo和LongMemEval两个基准测试中的实验表明，ByteRover在LoCoMo上达到当前最佳性能，在LongMemEval上也展现出具有竞争力的结果。更重要的是，该系统完全无需外部基础设施，不依赖向量数据库、图数据库或嵌入服务，所有知识均以人类可读的Markdown文件形式本地存储于文件系统中。

title: From Component Manipulation to System Compromise: Understanding and Detecting Malicious MCP Servers
authors: Yiheng Huang, Zhijia Zhao, Bihuan Chen, Susheng Wu, Zhuotong Zhou, Yiheng Cao, Xin Hu, Xin Peng
abstract: The model context protocol (MCP) standardizes how LLMs connect to external tools and data sources, enabling faster integration but introducing new attack vectors. Despite the growing adoption of MCP, existing MCP security studies classify attacks by their observable effects, obscuring how attacks behave across different MCP server components and overlooking multi-component attack chains. Meanwhile, existing defenses are less effective when facing multi-component attacks or previously unknown malicious behaviors. This work presents a component-centric perspective for understanding and detecting malicious MCP servers. First, we build the first component-centric PoC dataset of 114 malicious MCP servers where attacks are achieved as manipulation over MCP components and their compositions. We evaluate these attacks' effectiveness across two MCP hosts and five LLMs, and uncover that (1) component position shapes attack success rate; and (2) multi-component compositions often outperform single-component attacks by distributing malicious logic. Second, we propose and implement Connor, a two-stage behavioral deviation detector for malicious MCP servers. It first performs pre-execution analysis to detect malicious shell commands and extract each tool's function intent, and then conducts step-wise in-execution analysis to trace each tool's behavioral trajectories and detect deviations from its function intent. Evaluation on our curated dataset indicates that Connor achieves an F1-score of 94.6%, outperforming the state of the art by 8.9% to 59.6%. In real-world detection, Connor identifies two malicious servers.
title_zh: 从组件操控到系统沦陷：理解与检测恶意MCP服务器
abstract_zh: 模型上下文协议（MCP）标准化了大语言模型（LLMs）与外部工具及数据源的连接方式，虽加速了集成进程，但也引入了新的攻击路径。尽管MCP日益普及，现有安全研究仍主要依据攻击的可观测效果进行分类，这掩盖了攻击在不同MCP服务器组件中的实际行为特征，并忽略了多组件攻击链的存在。同时，现有防御机制在应对多组件攻击或未知恶意行为时表现不佳。本文提出一种以组件为中心的视角，用于理解和检测恶意MCP服务器。首先，我们构建了首个基于组件视角的PoC数据集，包含114个恶意MCP服务器实例，其中攻击通过操控MCP组件及其组合关系实现。我们在两个MCP主机和五个LLM上评估了这些攻击的有效性，发现：（1）组件的位置显著影响攻击成功率；（2）多组件组合攻击通常优于单一组件攻击，因其能更有效地分布恶意逻辑。其次，我们提出了并实现了Connor——一种两阶段的行为偏差检测系统，用于识别恶意MCP服务器。该系统首先在执行前分析潜在恶意Shell命令，并提取各工具的功能意图；随后在执行过程中进行分步分析，追踪每个工具的行为轨迹，并检测其是否偏离既定功能意图。在我们精心构建的数据集上的评估表明，Connor取得了94.6%的F1分数，相比当前最先进方法提升8.9%至59.6%。在真实场景检测中，Connor成功识别出两个恶意服务器。

title: DDCL-INCRT: A Self-Organising Transformer with Hierarchical Prototype Structure (Theoretical Foundations)
authors: Giansalvo Cirrincione
abstract: Modern neural networks of the transformer family require the practitioner to decide, before training begins, how many attention heads to use, how deep the network should be, and how wide each component should be. These decisions are made without knowledge of the task, producing architectures that are systematically larger than necessary: empirical studies find that a substantial fraction of heads and layers can be removed after training without performance loss. This paper introduces DDCL-INCRT, an architecture that determines its own structure during training. Two complementary ideas are combined. The first, DDCL (Deep Dual Competitive Learning), replaces the feedforward block with a dictionary of learned prototype vectors representing the most informative directions in the data. The prototypes spread apart automatically, driven by the training objective, without explicit regularisation. The second, INCRT (Incremental Transformer), controls the number of heads: starting from one, it adds a new head only when the directional information uncaptured by existing heads exceeds a threshold. The main theoretical finding is that these two mechanisms reinforce each other: each new head amplifies prototype separation, which in turn raises the signal triggering the next addition. At convergence, the network self-organises into a hierarchy of heads ordered by representational granularity. This hierarchical structure is proved to be unique and minimal, the smallest architecture sufficient for the task, under the stated conditions. Formal guarantees of stability, convergence, and pruning safety are established throughout. The architecture is not something one designs. It is something one derives.
title_zh: DDCL-INCRT：具有分层原型结构的自组织Transformer（理论基础）
abstract_zh: 现代的Transformer家族神经网络要求从业者在训练开始前就决定使用多少个注意力头、网络应有多深，以及每个组件应有多宽。这些决策都是在不了解具体任务的情况下做出的，导致所生成的架构往往远大于实际所需：实证研究表明，训练完成后，相当一部分注意力头和层都可以被移除而不会影响性能。本文提出了一种名为DDCL-INCRT的自适应架构，该架构能够在训练过程中自主确定自身的结构。这一方法结合了两个互补的思想。第一，DDCL（深度双重竞争学习）将传统的前馈模块替换为一组由数据中最具信息量的方向所学习得到的原型向量字典。这些原型向量会自动分散开来，完全由训练目标驱动，无需显式的正则化。第二，INCRT（增量式Transformer）动态控制注意力头的数量：从一个头开始，仅当现有头未能捕捉到的定向信息超过某个阈值时，才添加新的头。本文的主要理论发现是，这两种机制相互增强：每新增一个头都会强化原型之间的分离，而这种分离又提高了触发下一次添加的信号强度。在收敛时，网络会自我组织成一个按表征粒度排序的层次化头结构。该层次结构被证明在给定条件下是唯一且最小的——即完成任务所需的最简架构。文中还建立了关于稳定性、收敛性和剪枝安全性的严格形式保证。这种架构并非人为设计，而是通过推导得出。

title: From Guessing to Placeholding: A Cost-Theoretic Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Code Completion
authors: Liang Zhu, Haolin Chen, Lidong Zhao, Xian Wu
abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional proficiency in code completion, they typically adhere to a Hard Completion (HC) paradigm, compelling the generation of fully concrete code even amidst insufficient context. Our analysis of 3 million real-world interactions exposes the limitations of this strategy: 61% of the generated suggestions were either edited after acceptance or rejected despite exhibiting over 80% similarity to the user's subsequent code, suggesting that models frequently make erroneous predictions at specific token positions. Motivated by this observation, we propose Adaptive Placeholder Completion (APC), a collaborative framework that extends HC by strategically outputting explicit placeholders at high-entropy positions, allowing users to fill directly via IDE navigation. Theoretically, we formulate code completion as a cost-minimization problem under uncertainty. Premised on the observation that filling placeholders incurs lower cost than correcting errors, we prove the existence of a critical entropy threshold above which APC achieves strictly lower expected cost than HC. We instantiate this framework by constructing training data from filtered real-world edit logs and design a cost-based reward function for reinforcement learning. Extensive evaluations across 1.5B--14B parameter models demonstrate that APC reduces expected editing costs from 19% to 50% while preserving standard HC performance. Our work provides both a theoretical foundation and a practical training framework for uncertainty-aware code completion, demonstrating that adaptive abstention can be learned end-to-end without sacrificing conventional completion quality.
title_zh: 从猜测到占位：一种面向不确定性的代码补全成本理论框架
abstract_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码补全任务中表现出卓越的性能，但它们通常遵循“硬补全”（Hard Completion, HC）范式，即在上下文信息不足的情况下仍强制生成完全具体的代码。我们对300万次真实世界交互行为的分析揭示了这一策略的局限性：61%的生成建议在被接受后仍需修改，或虽与用户后续编写的代码相似度超过80%却被拒绝，这表明模型在特定标记位置频繁做出错误预测。基于这一发现，我们提出自适应占位符补全（Adaptive Placeholder Completion, APC），一种扩展HC范式的协作式框架。该框架在高熵位置有策略地输出显式占位符，允许用户通过IDE导航直接填充。理论上，我们将代码补全建模为不确定条件下的成本最小化问题。基于“填充占位符的成本低于修正错误”的观察，我们证明存在一个关键熵阈值，在此阈值以上，APC的期望成本严格低于传统HC。我们通过从筛选后的实际编辑日志中构建训练数据，并设计基于成本的奖励函数用于强化学习，实现了该框架的具体实例化。在参数量介于15亿至140亿的多种模型上进行的广泛评估表明，APC可将预期编辑成本降低50%至19%，同时保持与传统HC相当的完成质量。本研究为不确定性感知的代码补全提供了理论基础和实用的训练框架，证明了自适应“回避”策略可在不牺牲传统补全质量的前提下实现端到端学习。

title: Batched Contextual Reinforcement: A Task-Scaling Law for Efficient Reasoning
authors: Bangji Yang, Hongbo Ma, Jiajun Fan, Ge Liu
abstract: Large Language Models employing Chain-of-Thought reasoning achieve strong performance but suffer from excessive token consumption that inflates inference costs. Existing efficiency methods such as explicit length penalties, difficulty estimators, or multi-stage curricula either degrade reasoning quality or require complex training pipelines. We introduce Batched Contextual Reinforcement, a minimalist, single-stage training paradigm that unlocks efficient reasoning through a simple structural modification: training the model to solve N problems simultaneously within a shared context window, rewarded purely by per-instance accuracy. This formulation creates an implicit token budget that yields several key findings: (1) We identify a novel task-scaling law: as the number of concurrent problems N increases during inference, per-problem token usage decreases monotonically while accuracy degrades far more gracefully than baselines, establishing N as a controllable throughput dimension. (2) BCR challenges the traditional accuracy-efficiency trade-off by demonstrating a "free lunch" phenomenon at standard single-problem inference. Across both 1.5B and 4B model families, BCR reduces token usage by 15.8% to 62.6% while consistently maintaining or improving accuracy across five major mathematical benchmarks. (3) Qualitative analyses reveal emergent self-regulated efficiency, where models autonomously eliminate redundant metacognitive loops without explicit length supervision. (4) Crucially, we empirically demonstrate that implicit budget constraints successfully circumvent the adversarial gradients and catastrophic optimization collapse inherent to explicit length penalties, offering a highly stable, constraint-based alternative for length control. These results prove BCR practical, showing simple structural incentives unlock latent high-density reasoning in LLMs.
title_zh: 批量上下文强化：一种用于高效推理的任务扩展定律
abstract_zh:
采用思维链（Chain-of-Thought）推理的大语言模型虽然表现优异，但存在过度消耗token的问题，导致推理成本高昂。现有的效率优化方法，如显式的长度惩罚、难度估计器或多阶段教学课程，要么降低推理质量，要么需要复杂的训练流程。我们提出一种极简的单阶段训练范式——批量上下文强化（Batched Contextual Reinforcement, BCR），通过一个简单的结构改动实现高效推理：让模型在共享的上下文窗口中同时解决N个问题，并仅以每个实例的准确率为奖励信号进行训练。这一设定产生了一种隐式的token预算，带来了几个关键发现：

（1）我们发现了一种全新的任务扩展规律：在推理过程中，随着并发问题数N的增加，每个问题的token使用量呈单调递减趋势，而准确率下降却远比基线方法平缓，从而确立了N作为可调控的吞吐量维度。

（2）BCR打破了传统的准确率-效率权衡，在标准单问题推理场景下实现了“免费午餐”现象。在1.5B和4B两个模型系列中，BCR将token使用量减少了15.8%至62.6%，同时在五个主要数学基准测试上始终维持或提升了准确率。

（3）定性分析揭示出一种涌现的自我调节效率机制：模型无需显式的长度监督，便能自主消除冗余的认知循环，展现出高度自洽的推理行为。

（4）至关重要的是，我们实证表明，这种隐式预算约束能够有效规避显式长度惩罚所固有的对抗性梯度和灾难性优化崩溃问题，提供了一种高度稳定、基于约束的长度控制替代方案。

这些结果证明了BCR的实用性：仅通过简单的结构激励，即可激发大语言模型中潜藏的高密度推理能力。

title: TensorPool: A 3D-Stacked 8.4TFLOPS/4.3W Many-Core Domain-Specific Processor for AI-Native Radio Access Networks
authors: Marco Bertuletti, Yichao Zhang, Diyou Shen, Alessandro Vanelli-Coralli, Frank K. Gürkaynak, Luca Benini
abstract: The upcoming integration of AI in the physical layer (PHY) of 6G radio access networks (RAN) will enable a higher quality of service in challenging transmission scenarios. However, deeply optimized AI-Native PHY models impose higher computational complexity compared to conventional baseband, challenging deployment under the sub-msec real-time constraints typical of modern PHYs. Additionally, following the extension to terahertz carriers, the upcoming densification of 6G cell-sites further limits the power consumption of base stations, constraining the budget available for compute ($\leq$ 100W). The desired flexibility to ensure long term sustainability and the imperative energy-efficiency gains on the high-throughput tensor computations dominating AI-Native PHYs can be achieved by domain-specialization of many-core programmable baseband processors. Following the domain-specialization strategy, we present TensorPool, a cluster of 256 RISCV32IMAF programmable cores, accelerated by 16 256 MACs/cycle (FP16) tensor engines with low-latency access to 4MiB of L1 scratchpad for maximal data-reuse. Implemented in TSMC's N7, TensorPool achieves 3643~MACs/cycle (89% tensor-unit utilization) on tensor operations for AI-RAN, 6$\times$ more than a core-only cluster without tensor acceleration, while simultaneously improving GOPS/W/mm$^2$ efficiency by 9.1$\times$. Further, we show that 3D-stacking the computing blocks of TensorPool to better unfold the tensor engines to L1-memory routing provides 2.32$\times$ footprint improvement with no frequency degradation, compared to a 2D implementation.
title_zh: TensorPool：面向AI原生无线接入网的3D堆叠8.4TFLOPS/4.3W多核领域专用处理器
abstract_zh:
6G无线接入网络（RAN）物理层（PHY）中即将引入的人工智能（AI）技术，将在复杂传输场景下实现更高质量的服务。然而，深度优化的AI原生PHY模型相较于传统基带处理，带来了更高的计算复杂度，这在现代PHY普遍存在的亚毫秒级实时性约束下，给部署带来了挑战。此外，随着向太赫兹频段的扩展以及6G基站站点的进一步密集化，基站的功耗限制愈发严格，可分配的计算预算被压缩至不超过100W。为确保长期可持续性并实现高吞吐量张量运算中对能效的迫切提升，通过针对特定领域优化多核可编程基带处理器成为关键路径。

基于这一领域专用化策略，本文提出TensorPool：一个由256个RISC-V32IMAF可编程核心组成的集群，配备16个每周期可执行256个MAC操作（FP16精度）的张量加速引擎，并通过低延迟访问4MiB的L1暂存内存，以实现最大化的数据重用。该设计采用台积电N7工艺实现，在AI-RAN的张量运算中达到3643 MAC/周期（张量单元利用率高达89%），相比仅依赖核心的集群（无张量加速）性能提升6倍；同时，其GOPSW/mm²能效比也提升了9.1倍。此外，我们还展示了将TensorPool的计算模块进行3D堆叠，以更高效地展开张量引擎与L1内存之间的路由连接，相较传统的2D实现，可在不降低频率的前提下实现2.32倍的面积缩减。

title: Neuro-RIT: Neuron-Guided Instruction Tuning for Robust Retrieval-Augmented Language Model
authors: Jaemin Kim, Jae O Lee, Sumyeong Ahn, Seo Yeon Park
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Language Models (RALMs) have demonstrated significant potential in knowledge-intensive tasks; however, they remain vulnerable to performance degradation when presented with irrelevant or noisy retrieved contexts. Existing approaches to enhance robustness typically operate via coarse-grained parameter updates at the layer or module level, often overlooking the inherent neuron-level sparsity of Large Language Models (LLMs). To address this limitation, we propose Neuro-RIT (Neuron-guided Robust Instruction Tuning), a novel framework that shifts the paradigm from dense adaptation to precision-driven neuron alignment. Our method explicitly disentangles neurons that are responsible for processing relevant versus irrelevant contexts using attribution-based neuron mining. Subsequently, we introduce a two-stage instruction tuning strategy that enforces a dual capability for noise robustness: achieving direct noise suppression by functionally deactivating neurons exclusive to irrelevant contexts, while simultaneously optimizing targeted layers for evidence distillation. Extensive experiments across diverse QA benchmarks demonstrate that Neuro-RIT consistently outperforms strong baselines and robustness-enhancing methods.
title_zh: Neuro-RIT：基于神经元引导的指令微调以实现鲁棒的检索增强型语言模型
abstract_zh: 检索增强型语言模型（Retrieval-Augmented Language Models, RALMs）在知识密集型任务中展现出巨大潜力，但当面对无关或噪声干扰的检索上下文时，其性能仍易下降。现有提升鲁棒性的方法通常通过粗粒度的参数更新（如在层或模块层面）实现，往往忽略了大型语言模型（LLM）固有的神经元级稀疏性。为解决这一局限，我们提出一种名为Neuro-RIT（神经元引导的鲁棒指令微调）的新框架，将范式从密集适应转向以精准为导向的神经元对齐。我们的方法通过基于归因的神经元挖掘，明确分离出负责处理相关与无关上下文的神经元。随后，我们设计了一种两阶段指令微调策略，强化模型对噪声的鲁棒性：一方面通过功能上关闭仅响应无关上下文的神经元，实现直接的噪声抑制；另一方面同时优化特定层，以促进有效证据的提炼。在多种问答基准上的大量实验表明，Neuro-RIT始终优于现有的强基线及各类鲁棒性增强方法。

title: NED-Tree: Bridging the Semantic Gap with Nonlinear Element Decomposition Tree for LLM Nonlinear Optimization Modeling
authors: Zhijing Hu, Yufan Deng, Haoyang Liu, Changjun Fan
abstract: Automating the translation of Operations Research (OR) problems from natural language to executable models is a critical challenge. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise in linear tasks, they suffer from severe performance degradation in real-world nonlinear scenarios due to semantic misalignment between mathematical formulations and solver codes, as well as unstable information extraction. In this study, we introduce NED-Tree, a systematic framework designed to bridge the semantic gap. NED-Tree employs (a) a sentence-by-sentence extraction strategy to ensure robust parameter mapping and traceability; and (b) a recursive tree-based structure that adaptively decomposes complex nonlinear terms into solver-compatible sub-elements. Additionally, we present NEXTOR, a novel benchmark specifically designed for complex nonlinear, extensive-constraint OR problems. Experiments across 10 benchmarks demonstrate that NED-Tree establishes a new state-of-the-art with 72.51% average accuracy, NED-Tree is the first framework that drives LLMs to resolve nonlinear modeling difficulties through element decomposition, achieving alignment between modeling semantics and code semantics. The NED-Tree framework and benchmark are accessible in the anonymous repository https://anonymous.4open.science/r/NORA-NEXTOR.
title_zh: NED-Tree：通过非线性元素分解树弥合大语言模型非线性优化建模中的语义鸿沟
abstract_zh: 将运筹学（OR）问题从自然语言自动翻译为可执行模型是一项关键挑战。尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在处理线性任务方面展现出潜力，但在真实世界中的非线性场景下，其性能会严重下降，原因在于数学表达式与求解器代码之间存在语义错位，以及信息提取的不稳定性。在本研究中，我们提出了NED-Tree——一种系统化框架，旨在弥合这一语义鸿沟。NED-Tree采用：(a) 逐句提取策略，以确保参数映射的鲁棒性和可追溯性；以及 (b) 递归树状结构，能够自适应地将复杂的非线性项分解为求解器兼容的子元素。此外，我们还提出了NEXTOR——一个专为复杂非线性、大规模约束运筹学问题设计的新基准。在10个基准上的实验表明，NED-Tree实现了72.51%的平均准确率，创下新纪录。NED-Tree是首个通过元素分解引导LLMs解决非线性建模难题的框架，成功实现了建模语义与代码语义的一致性。NED-Tree框架及基准数据集可在匿名仓库 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/NORA-NEXTOR 中获取。

title: Are Benchmark Tests Strong Enough? Mutation-Guided Diagnosis and Augmentation of Regression Suites
authors: Chenglin Li, Yisen Xu, Zehao Wang, Shin Hwei Tan, Tse-Hsun, Chen
abstract: Benchmarks driven by test suites, notably SWE-bench, have become the de facto standard for measuring the effectiveness of automated issue-resolution agents: a generated patch is accepted whenever it passes the accompanying regression tests. In practice, however, insufficiently strong test suites can admit plausible yet semantically incorrect patches, inflating reported success rates. We introduce STING, a framework for targeted test augmentation that uses semantically altered program variants as diagnostic stressors to uncover and repair weaknesses in benchmark regression suites. Variants of the ground-truth patch that still pass the existing tests reveal under-constrained behaviors; these gaps then guide the generation of focused regression tests. A generated test is retained only if it (i) passes on the ground-truth patch, (ii) fails on at least one variant that survived the original suite, and (iii) remains valid under behavior-preserving transformations designed to guard against overfitting. Applied to SWE-bench Verified, STING finds that 77% of instances contain at least one surviving variant. STING produces 1,014 validated tests spanning 211 instances and increases patch-region line and branch coverage by 10.8% and 9.5%, respectively. Re-assessing the top-10 repair agents with the strengthened suites lowers their resolved rates by 4.2%-9.0%, revealing that a substantial share of previously passing patches exploit weaknesses in the benchmark tests rather than faithfully implementing the intended fix. These results underscore that reliable benchmark evaluation depends not only on patch generation, but equally on test adequacy.
title_zh: 基准测试足够强大吗？基于突变的回归测试诊断与增强
abstract_zh: 由测试套件驱动的基准测试，尤其是SWE-bench，已成为衡量自动化问题修复代理有效性的事实标准：只要生成的补丁通过了配套的回归测试，即被视为成功。然而在实践中，测试套件若不够充分，可能会允许看似合理但语义错误的补丁通过，从而虚高报告的成功率。我们提出了STING——一种面向目标的测试增强框架，该框架利用语义上被修改的程序变体作为诊断性压力测试，以发现并修复基准回归测试套件中的薄弱环节。那些仍能通过现有测试的真值补丁变体，揭示出测试的约束不足；这些漏洞随后指导生成针对性的回归测试。一个生成的测试仅在满足以下三个条件时才被保留：(i) 在真值补丁上通过，(ii) 在至少一个原测试套件中存活下来的变体上失败，(iii) 在保持行为不变的变换下依然有效，以防止过拟合。将STING应用于SWE-bench Verified，我们发现77%的实例中至少存在一个存活的变体。STING共生成了1,014个经过验证的测试，覆盖211个实例，使补丁所在区域的行覆盖率和分支覆盖率分别提升了10.8%和9.5%。使用强化后的测试套件重新评估表现最佳的10个修复代理，其修复成功率下降了4.2%至9.0%，表明此前大量“通过”的补丁实际上利用了基准测试中的缺陷，而非真正实现了预期修复。这些结果强调，可靠的基准评估不仅依赖于补丁生成能力，同样取决于测试用例的充分性。

title: Bridging Large-Model Reasoning and Real-Time Control via Agentic Fast-Slow Planning
authors: Jiayi Chen, Shuai Wang, Guangxu Zhu, Chengzhong Xu
abstract: Large foundation models enable powerful reasoning for autonomous systems, but mapping semantic intent to reliable real-time control remains challenging. Existing approaches either (i) let Large Language Models (LLMs) generate trajectories directly - brittle, hard to verify, and latency-prone - or (ii) adjust Model Predictive Control (MPC) objectives online - mixing slow deliberation with fast control and blurring interfaces. We propose Agentic Fast-Slow Planning, a hierarchical framework that decouples perception, reasoning, planning, and control across natural timescales. The framework contains two bridges. Perception2Decision compresses scenes into ego-centric topologies using an on-vehicle Vision-Language Model (VLM) detector, then maps them to symbolic driving directives in the cloud with an LLM decision maker - reducing bandwidth and delay while preserving interpretability. Decision2Trajectory converts directives into executable paths: Semantic-Guided A* embeds language-derived soft costs into classical search to bias solutions toward feasible trajectories, while an Agentic Refinement Module adapts planner hyperparameters using feedback and memory. Finally, MPC tracks the trajectories in real time, with optional cloud-guided references for difficult cases. Experiments in CARLA show that Agentic Fast-Slow Planning improves robustness under perturbations, reducing lateral deviation by up to 45% and completion time by over 12% compared to pure MPC and an A*-guided MPC baseline. Code is available at https://github.com/cjychenjiayi/icra2026_AFSP.
title_zh: 通过智能体快速-慢速规划实现大模型推理与实时控制的融合
abstract_zh:
大型基础模型为自主系统提供了强大的推理能力，但将语义意图映射为可靠且实时的控制仍具挑战性。现有方法要么（i）让大型语言模型（LLMs）直接生成轨迹——这种方式脆弱、难以验证且存在延迟问题；要么（ii）在线调整模型预测控制（MPC）目标——将缓慢的决策过程与快速的控制行为混合在一起，导致系统边界模糊。我们提出了一种**代理式快慢规划**（Agentic Fast-Slow Planning）框架，通过自然的时间尺度将感知、推理、规划与控制解耦。该框架包含两个关键桥梁：  
**感知→决策**（Perception2Decision）利用车载视觉-语言模型（VLM）检测器，将场景压缩为以自身为中心的拓扑结构，随后由云端的LLM决策模块将其转化为符号化的驾驶指令——在降低带宽和延迟的同时保持可解释性。  
**决策→轨迹**（Decision2Trajectory）则将指令转化为可执行路径：**语义引导的A\***算法将语言推导出的软约束融入经典搜索过程，引导求解偏向可行轨迹；而**代理式精炼模块**则通过反馈与记忆动态调整规划器超参数。最后，MPC在实时层面跟踪轨迹，并在复杂情况下可选地引入云端指导参考。  
在CARLA平台上的实验表明，与纯MPC及基于A\*引导的MPC基线相比，代理式快慢规划显著提升了系统鲁棒性，在扰动条件下使横向偏差最多减少45%，完成时间缩短超过12%。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/cjychenjiayi/icra2026_AFSP。

title: ContextBudget: Budget-Aware Context Management for Long-Horizon Search Agents
authors: Yong Wu, YanZhao Zheng, TianZe Xu, ZhenTao Zhang, YuanQiang Yu, JiHuai Zhu, Chao Ma, BinBin Lin, BaoHua Dong, HangCheng Zhu, RuoHui Huang, Gang Yu
abstract: LLM-based agents show strong potential for long-horizon reasoning, yet their context size is limited by deployment factors (e.g., memory, latency, and cost), yielding a constrained context budget. As interaction histories grow, this induces a trade-off between retaining past information and staying within the context limit. To address this challenge, we propose Budget-Aware Context Management (BACM), which formulates context management as a sequential decision problem with a context budget constraint. It enables agents to assess the available budget before incorporating new observations and decide when and how much of the interaction history to compress. We further develop BACM-RL, an end-to-end curriculum-based reinforcement learning approach that learns compression strategies under varying context budgets. Experiments on compositional multi-objective QA and long-horizon web browsing benchmarks show that BACM-RL consistently outperforms prior methods across model scales and task complexities, achieving over $1.6\times$ gains over strong baselines in high-complexity settings, while maintaining strong advantages as budgets shrink, where most methods exhibit a downward performance trend.
title_zh: 预算感知的上下文管理用于长时序搜索代理
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体在长时序推理方面展现出巨大潜力，但其上下文长度受限于部署因素（如内存、延迟和成本），导致可用的上下文预算十分有限。随着交互历史的不断积累，如何在保留过往信息与遵守上下文限制之间取得平衡，成为一项关键挑战。为应对这一问题，我们提出了**预算感知的上下文管理机制**（Budget-Aware Context Management, BACM），将上下文管理建模为一个带有上下文预算约束的序列决策问题。该方法使智能体能够在引入新观测前评估当前可用预算，并自主决定何时以及以何种程度对交互历史进行压缩。为进一步提升性能，我们还设计了BACM-RL——一种端到端的、基于课程学习的强化学习方法，能够学习在不同上下文预算下有效的压缩策略。在组合多目标问答和长时序网页浏览等基准任务上的实验表明，BACM-RL在多种模型规模和任务复杂度下均显著优于现有方法，在高复杂度场景中相比强基线实现超过1.6倍的性能提升；同时，当上下文预算逐渐缩减时，BACM-RL仍保持显著优势，而大多数现有方法则表现出明显的性能下降趋势。

title: From SWE-ZERO to SWE-HERO: Execution-free to Execution-based Fine-tuning for Software Engineering Agents
authors: Nikolai Ludwig, Wasi Uddin Ahmad, Somshubra Majumdar, Boris Ginsburg
abstract: We introduce SWE-ZERO to SWE-HERO, a two-stage SFT recipe that achieves state-of-the-art results on SWE-bench by distilling open-weight frontier LLMs. Our pipeline replaces resource-heavy dependencies with an evolutionary refinement strategy: (1) SWE-ZERO utilizes large-scale, execution-free trajectories to master code semantics and repository-level reasoning, and (2) SWE-HERO applies targeted, execution-backed refinement to transition these semantic intuitions into rigorous engineering workflows. Our empirical results set a new benchmark for open-source models of comparable size. We release a dataset of 300k SWE-ZERO and 13k SWE-HERO trajectories distilled from Qwen3-Coder-480B, alongside a suite of agents based on the Qwen2.5-Coder series. Notably, SWE-HERO-32B achieves a 62.2% resolution rate on SWE-bench Verified. Furthermore, despite being trained exclusively on Python, our agents demonstrate robust zero-shot transferability on SWE-bench Multilingual, reaching 44.1% and confirming the paradigm's generalizability across diverse languages.
title_zh: 从 SWE-ZERO 到 SWE-HERO：面向软件工程智能体的无执行微调到基于执行的微调
abstract_zh: 我们提出 SWE-ZERO 到 SWE-HERO 的两阶段监督微调（SFT）方案，通过蒸馏开源前沿大模型，在 SWE-bench 上取得了当前最优性能。我们的流程摒弃了资源密集型依赖，转而采用一种进化式精炼策略：(1) SWE-ZERO 利用大规模、无需执行的轨迹，掌握代码语义与仓库级推理能力；(2) SWE-HERO 通过有针对性的、基于执行反馈的迭代优化，将这些语义直觉转化为严谨的工程实践流程。实验结果为同规模开源模型树立了新的基准。我们公开发布了由 Qwen3-Coder-480B 蒸馏得到的 30 万条 SWE-ZERO 轨迹和 1.3 万条 SWE-HERO 轨迹数据集，并配套提供基于 Qwen2.5-Coder 系列构建的一系列智能体。值得注意的是，SWE-HERO-32B 在 SWE-bench Verified 上实现了 62.2% 的问题解决率。此外，尽管训练仅限于 Python，我们的智能体在 SWE-bench Multilingual 上仍展现出强大的零样本迁移能力，达到 44.1% 的表现，充分验证了该范式的跨语言通用性。

title: Performance Analysis of Compiler Code Generation for HPC and AI Kernels on RISC-V
authors: Rama Kishan Malladi, Nitya Hariharan
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: The open-source RISC-V architecture [1] is well suited for systems that run diverse applications such as HPC, AI, IoT and embedded systems. Performance analysis of various kernels on a RISC-V architecture is necessary while making a choice of an implementation. In the absence of hardware, the LLVM-MCA [2] tool can provide useful information on the implementation of the RISC-V architecture and its impact on the performance of HPC and AI kernels. In this paper, we use the LLVM-MCA tool to analyze the performance of various HPC and AI kernels on a RISC-V implementation by Tenstorrent<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup>. We look at the performance of these workloads on this RISC-V platform, and discuss the various considerations that determine runtime performance.
title_zh: RISC-V 上高性能计算与人工智能核函数的编译器代码生成性能分析
abstract_zh: 开源的RISC-V架构[1]非常适合运行多种应用，如高性能计算（HPC）、人工智能（AI）、物联网（IoT）和嵌入式系统。在选择实现方案时，对RISC-V架构上各类内核的性能进行分析是必要的。在缺乏实际硬件的情况下，LLVM-MCA[2]工具能够提供有关RISC-V架构实现及其对HPC和AI内核性能影响的有用信息。本文利用LLVM-MCA工具，分析Tenstorrent<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">*</sup>实现的RISC-V平台上各种HPC和AI内核的性能表现。我们考察了这些工作负载在该RISC-V平台上的性能表现，并讨论了决定运行时性能的各种因素。

title: A Framework of Automated LC -VCO Design with Physical Layout Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sungjin Kim, Hyunsoo Lee, Seongmin Hong, Heein Yoon, Taigon Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been increasingly utilized across various fields, including communications, healthcare, and Computer-Aided Design (CAD). However, AI has shown relatively limited advances in analog and RF circuit design, which are critical for modern communication systems such as 5G due to their higher complexity and nonlinear characteristics. For example, the Inductor-Capacitor Voltage-Controlled Oscillator (LC-VCO) is a crucial component in frequency synthesizers, determining the performance of RF systems, including high data transmission rates and wide bandwidth. In fact, LC-VCO design is challenging due to the high parameter variability and complex interactions between design variables, making it challenging to optimize parameters to meet target specifications. Thus, this study proposes a comprehensive LC-VCO design methodology compatible across multiple process nodes and supports optimization down to the layout level. We use Reinforcement Learning (RL) to navigate the nonlinear design space efficiently for schematic optimization and apply an algorithm from Gradient Descent to optimize the design at the physical layout level. We highlight that the versatility of our methodology is demonstrated by producing optimized Figures of Merit (FoM) across various technology nodes and frequency ranges, showcasing its potential as a universal design tool accessible to all users.
title_zh: 基于强化学习的物理版图自动化LC-VCO设计框架
abstract_zh:
人工智能（AI）在通信、医疗以及计算机辅助设计（CAD）等多个领域得到了日益广泛的应用。然而，在模拟与射频（RF）电路设计方面，AI的应用进展相对有限，这主要是因为这类电路具有更高的复杂性及非线性特性，而这些特性在现代通信系统（如5G）中至关重要。例如，电感-电容压控振荡器（LC-VCO）是频率合成器中的关键组件，直接决定了射频系统的性能，包括高速数据传输速率和宽频带能力。事实上，LC-VCO的设计极具挑战性，主要由于设计参数的高变异性以及各设计变量之间复杂的相互作用，使得在满足目标规格的前提下进行参数优化十分困难。

因此，本研究提出了一种全面的LC-VCO设计方法，该方法兼容多种工艺节点，并支持从电路图到版图级别的优化。我们采用强化学习（RL）技术高效探索非线性设计空间，以实现电路图层面的优化；同时，运用基于梯度下降的算法对物理版图层面的设计进行优化。研究结果表明，该方法具有高度的通用性，能够在不同技术节点和频率范围内生成优化的性能指标（FoM），充分展示了其作为通用设计工具的巨大潜力，可为各类用户所便捷使用。

title: Leveraging AI Tools in Engineering Education: Promise and Pitfalls of AI in Software Development
authors: Antonio Carlos Bento, José Reinaldo Silva, Marcos Ribeiro Pereira Barretto, Sérgio Camacho-León, Elsa Yolanda Torres-Torres
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: This paper examines the pedagogical impact of Artificial Intelligence-assisted tools in a university software construction course. Through a 10-week case study with 25 engineering students, the study captures qualitative insights into how students interact with AI tools, the challenges they face, and the learning strategies that emerge. While students reported perceived time savings, the core contribution lies in understanding the educational implications, both positive and challenging of integrating AI into project-based learning environments. The findings reveal that Artificial Intelligence tools enhance prototyping and debugging skills but require structured integration to mitigate over-reliance. The study contributes a framework for balancing Artificial Intelligence use with traditional pedagogy, supporting Sustainable Development Goals 4’s goal of equitable education, while the opinion survey carried out with students shows a result of 96% satisfaction with the use of assisted artificial intelligence tools during the development of the project during the studies.
title_zh: 在工程教育中利用人工智能工具：人工智能在软件开发中的前景与风险
abstract_zh: 本文探讨了人工智能辅助工具在大学软件构建课程中的教学影响。通过为期10周、涵盖25名工程专业学生的案例研究，该研究深入捕捉了学生与AI工具互动的定性见解，揭示了他们所面临的挑战以及由此产生的学习策略。尽管学生普遍报告称使用AI节省了时间，但本研究的核心贡献在于深入理解将AI融入项目式学习环境所带来的教育意义，包括积极影响与潜在挑战。研究发现，人工智能工具有助于提升原型设计与调试能力，但需通过结构化整合来避免过度依赖。本研究提出了一种平衡人工智能应用与传统教学法的框架，支持可持续发展目标4中关于公平教育的目标。此外，针对学生开展的意见调查结果显示，96%的学生对在学习过程中使用人工智能辅助工具进行项目开发表示满意。

title: GPU Kernel-Level Characterization and Optimization of Transformer Models: From Baseline to LoRA Fine-Tuning
authors: Sasi Snigdha Yadavalli, Badrinath Ramamurthy
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This study addresses the problem of systematically applying hardware-software optimizations to standard transformer training pipelines. The investigation examines how hardware-aware GPU techniques and LoRA hyperparameters jointly influence computational efficiency and model quality. By profiling CUDA kernel-level metrics and model-level scores (BLEU, chrF), we demonstrate how optimizations like FlashAttention and batched memory operations reduce execution time, while LoRA hyperparameters affect both model quality and computational load.
title_zh: GPU内核级Transformer模型表征与优化：从基础模型到LoRA微调
abstract_zh: 本研究针对标准Transformer训练流程中系统性地应用软硬件优化的问题展开探讨。研究分析了硬件感知的GPU技术与LoRA超参数如何共同影响计算效率和模型质量。通过分析CUDA内核级别的指标以及模型级别的评分（如BLEU、chrF），我们证明了FlashAttention和批处理内存操作等优化手段能够有效降低执行时间，而LoRA超参数则同时影响模型质量和计算负载。

title: Accelerating Triton convolutions by exposing Shared Memory to the Programmer
authors: Shourya Goel, Ricky Dev, Pradeep Ramachandran
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: With each new generation of NVIDIA GPUs, advancements in memory bandwidth, tensor core throughput, and specialized hardware features necessitate significant redevelopment of CUDA kernels to maximize deep learning model performance. This process is resource-intensive and often results in brittle, hardware-specific code that is challenging to maintain. Triton, a Python-like domain-specific language (DSL), addresses these challenges by offering a block-based programming API that abstracts the complexities of CUDA kernel development.
title_zh: 通过向程序员暴露共享内存来加速Triton卷积运算
abstract_zh: 随着每一代NVIDIA GPU的推出，内存带宽、张量核心吞吐量以及专用硬件功能的提升，都要求对CUDA内核进行大规模重构，以充分发挥深度学习模型的性能。这一过程耗时耗力，且常常导致代码脆弱、高度依赖特定硬件，难以维护。Triton是一种类似Python的领域特定语言（DSL），通过提供基于块的编程API，抽象了CUDA内核开发的复杂性，有效解决了这些问题。

title: Leveraging Key Source Code Metrics for Predicting Software Vulnerabilities with Machine Learning
authors: Behzad Khalaji Emamzadeh Abbasi, Alireza Shameli-Sendi
abstract: Security vulnerabilities are a common concern in software systems, posing significant risks such as unauthorized access, data breaches, and system failures, which makes their early detection essential for both organizations and end-users. To address this challenge, various techniques—particularly machine learning—have been explored. This study investigates the key source code factors that contribute to software vulnerability prediction using multiple machine learning models, with the dual objective of detecting vulnerability presence and classifying vulnerability types such as Denial of Service and Buffer Overflow. Unlike prior studies that largely overlook the relationship between code metrics and specific vulnerability categories, this work systematically analyzes these connections to provide actionable security insights. The proposed approach is evaluated on datasets from ten real-world projects, including OpenSSL, SQLite, FreeType, LibTIFF, Libxslt, Binutils, FFmpeg, ImageMagick, OpenSC, and rdesktop, while addressing class imbalance through the SmoteTomek technique. Experimental results demonstrate that Random Forest achieves superior performance, reaching an accuracy of 0.96, precision of 0.948, recall of 0.974, and F1-score of 0.961 in vulnerability detection, along with strong effectiveness in vulnerability type classification. Overall, the findings highlight the most influential source code metrics in vulnerability prediction and confirm the practical value of machine learning for enhancing secure software development.
title_zh: 利用关键源代码度量指标通过机器学习预测软件漏洞
abstract_zh: 安全漏洞是软件系统中常见的关注点，可能带来未经授权的访问、数据泄露和系统故障等重大风险，因此早期检测对于组织和最终用户而言至关重要。为应对这一挑战，研究人员探索了多种技术，尤其是机器学习方法。本研究利用多种机器学习模型，深入分析影响软件漏洞预测的关键源代码特征，旨在实现漏洞存在性检测以及漏洞类型分类（如拒绝服务攻击和缓冲区溢出）。与以往大多忽视代码度量与特定漏洞类别之间关系的研究不同，本文系统地分析了这些关联，以提供可操作的安全洞察。所提出的方案在十个真实世界项目的数据集上进行了评估，包括 OpenSSL、SQLite、FreeType、LibTIFF、Libxslt、Binutils、FFmpeg、ImageMagick、OpenSC 和 rdesktop，并通过 SmoteTomek 技术有效缓解了类别不平衡问题。实验结果表明，随机森林模型表现优异，在漏洞检测任务中达到了 0.96 的准确率、0.948 的精确率、0.974 的召回率和 0.961 的 F1 分数，同时在漏洞类型分类方面也展现出强大的效果。总体而言，研究结果揭示了对漏洞预测最具影响力的源代码度量指标，验证了机器学习在提升安全软件开发实践中的实际价值。

title: Review of Superconducting Qubit Devices and Their Large-Scale Integration
authors: Hiu Yung Wong
abstract: The superconducting qubit quantum computer is one of the most promising quantum computing architectures for large-scale integration due to its maturity and close proximity to the well-established semiconductor manufacturing infrastructure. From an education perspective, it also bridges classical microwave electronics and quantum electrodynamics. In this paper, we will review the basics of quantum computers, superconductivity, and Josephson junctions. We then introduce important technologies and concepts related to DiVincenzo’s criteria, which are the necessary conditions for the superconducting qubit technology to work as a useful quantum computer. Firstly, we will discuss various types of superconducting qubits formed with Josephson junctions, from which we will understand the trade-off across multiple design parameters, including their noise immunity. Secondly, we will discuss different schemes to achieve entanglement gate operations, which are a major bottleneck in achieving more efficient fault-tolerant quantum computing. Thirdly, we will review readout engineering, including the implementations of the Purcell filters and quantum-limited amplifiers. Finally, we will discuss the nature and review the studies of two-level system defects, which are currently the limiting factor of qubit coherence time. DiVincenzo’s criteria are only the necessary conditions for a technology to be eligible for quantum computing. To have a useful quantum computer, large-scale integration is required. We will review proposals and developments for the large-scale integration of superconducting qubit devices. By comparing with the application of electronic design automation (EDA) in semiconductors, we will also review the use of EDA in superconducting qubit quantum computer design, which is necessary for its large-scale integration.
title_zh: 超导量子比特器件及其大规模集成的综述
abstract_zh: 超导量子比特量子计算机因其成熟度以及与成熟的半导体制造基础设施的紧密联系，成为大规模集成中最具前景的量子计算架构之一。从教育角度来看，它还架起了经典微波电子学与量子电动力学之间的桥梁。本文将回顾量子计算机、超导性以及约瑟夫森结的基本原理。随后，我们将介绍与迪文森佐（DiVincenzo）标准相关的重要技术与概念，这些是超导量子比特技术作为实用量子计算机所必需满足的条件。首先，我们将讨论由约瑟夫森结构成的各种类型的超导量子比特，从而理解在多个设计参数之间的权衡关系，包括其抗噪声能力。其次，我们将探讨实现纠缠门操作的不同方案，这是实现更高效容错量子计算的主要瓶颈。第三，我们将回顾读出工程方面的进展，包括Purcell滤波器和量子极限放大器的实现。最后，我们将讨论两级系统缺陷的本质，并综述当前关于此类缺陷的研究，因为它们目前是限制量子比特相干时间的主要因素。需要指出的是，迪文森佐标准仅是某项技术具备量子计算潜力的必要条件。要构建真正有用的量子计算机，必须实现大规模集成。本文还将回顾超导量子比特器件大规模集成的相关设想与进展。通过与半导体领域中电子设计自动化（EDA）的应用进行比较，我们还将探讨EDA在超导量子比特量子计算机设计中的应用，这对于其实现大规模集成至关重要。

title: Towards an Agentic AI Design across Edge and Cloud
authors: Shiva Sai Krishna Anand Tokal, Vaibhav Jha, Anand Eswaran, Praveen Jayachandran, Yogesh Simmhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: AI agents typically rely on massive LLMs which are associated with high resource demands and inference costs. This limits the deployment of complex agentic systems on edge devices with limited computational power. Furthermore, existing state-of-the-art agentic workflow patterns often struggle with long context management leading to excessive token usage and increased cost which becomes a problem particularly for smaller models with limited context windows. We define a novel agentic workflow pattern specifically designed for context window efficiency. It decomposes the task into stages to create opportunities for context optimization and efficient information retention across multiple steps of the application. This approach aims to significantly minimizes redundant context, enabling the execution of larger, multi-step agentic applications without exceeding the token limits of Small Language Models (SLMs).
title_zh: 面向边缘与云协同的智能体AI设计
abstract_zh: AI代理通常依赖于大规模语言模型（LLMs），而这些模型伴随着高昂的资源消耗和推理成本，限制了复杂代理系统在计算能力有限的边缘设备上的部署。此外，现有的最先进代理工作流模式在处理长上下文时往往表现不佳，导致令牌使用量过大、成本增加，这对上下文窗口较小的模型而言尤为突出。为此，我们提出了一种专为提升上下文窗口效率而设计的新颖代理工作流模式。该模式将任务分解为多个阶段，从而创造优化上下文和跨多步骤高效保留信息的机会。这一方法旨在显著减少冗余上下文，使更大规模、多步骤的代理应用能够在不超出小型语言模型（SLMs）令牌限制的前提下顺利执行。

title: PARISCV: A RISC-V Profiler for Application-Specific Hardware Optimization
authors: Spoorthi S, M. R. Ashuthosh, Bharadhwaj K, Vinay Reddy, Madhura Purnaprajna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: The growing need for faster and more reliable computing has increased the use of application-specific architectures. RISC-V, with its simplicity and modularity, is an ideal platform for such design exploration. Application performance can be improved by adding ISA extensions, but while hardware implementations reduce execution cycles, they also increase area. Finding the right balance between software cycles and hardware cost is therefore crucial. PARISCV is a profiling tool that analyzes RISC-V execution traces to identify beneficial ISA extensions and potential custom instructions. It streamlines performance tuning by highlighting opportunities for acceleration. PARISCV’s extension recommendations achieved up to a 1.4× speed-up on the CV32E40X core with an area overhead of 1.15× and power overhead of 1.01×, while applications that did not require the B extension saw no improvement.
title_zh: PARISCV：面向特定应用硬件优化的RISC-V性能分析器
abstract_zh: 日益增长的对更快、更可靠计算的需求，推动了专用架构的广泛应用。RISC-V凭借其简洁性和模块化特性，成为此类设计探索的理想平台。通过添加指令集架构（ISA）扩展，可以提升应用性能，但尽管硬件实现能减少执行周期，也会增加面积开销。因此，在软件执行周期与硬件成本之间找到恰当的平衡至关重要。PARISCV是一款分析RISC-V执行轨迹的剖析工具，能够识别出有益的ISA扩展和潜在的自定义指令。它通过突出显示加速机会，简化了性能调优过程。在CV32E40X核心上，PARISCV的扩展建议实现了最高达1.4倍的加速，同时仅带来1.15倍的面积开销和1.01倍的功耗开销；而对于不需要B扩展的应用，则未见性能提升。

title: Hardware-Software Co-Design of Post-Quantum FALCON Digital Signature Scheme with RISC-V
authors: Rishabh Shrivastava, Utsav Banerjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: FALCON is a lattice-based digital signature scheme recently selected as NIST post-quantum cryptography standard. FALCON is well known for its fast verification support along with small key and signature sizes. However, implementation of the FALCON signature algorithm in resource-constrained edge computing systems is challenging due to the requirement of complex hashing, floating point arithmetic and polynomial transformation computations. In this work, we demonstrate resource-efficient hardware acceleration of FALCON signature generation and verification by performing hardware-software co-design of its computationally expensive functions with a light-weight 32-bit Vex RISC-V processor core with accompanying custom instructions. We propose Custom Function Units compatible with Vex RISC-V to accelerate Keccak permutations and polynomial transformations, along with a constant-time implementation of IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic. Our design achieves ≈ 4.3× speedup for signature verification and ≈ 1.6× speedup for signature generation compared to the baseline software on Vex RISC-V for both parameter sets of FALCON, when implemented on AMD Xilinx UltraScale+ FPGA. We also present an efficient verification-only variant of our design suitable for resource-constrained applications.
title_zh: 基于RISC-V的后量子FALCON数字签名方案的软硬件协同设计
abstract_zh: FALCON是一种最近被NIST选为后量子密码学标准的基于格的数字签名方案。FALCON以其快速的验证支持以及较小的密钥和签名尺寸而著称。然而，由于其对复杂哈希、浮点运算及多项式变换计算的需求，将FALCON签名算法部署在资源受限的边缘计算系统中面临挑战。本文通过针对计算开销较大的函数进行软硬件协同设计，利用轻量级32位Vex RISC-V处理器核心及其配套的自定义指令，实现了FALCON签名生成与验证的高效硬件加速。我们提出了与Vex RISC-V兼容的自定义功能单元，用于加速Keccak置换和多项式变换，并实现了一种符合IEEE 754标准的恒定时间浮点数运算。在AMD Xilinx UltraScale+ FPGA上实现时，该设计相较于基准软件在Vex RISC-V上的表现，对FALCON的两种参数集均实现了约4.3倍的签名验证加速和约1.6倍的签名生成加速。此外，我们还提出了一种高效的仅支持验证的优化版本，适用于资源受限的应用场景。

title: MATANALYZE - A Custom Accelerator Generator for Sparse × Dense and Dense × Sparse Matrix Multiplication
authors: Mohammed Sahel, Disha Kiran D, M. R. Ashuthosh, Madhura Purnaprajna
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Matrix multiplication is fundamental to deep learning, scientific computation, and graph analytics. The prevalence of sparse matrices in these fields offers significant opportunities to reduce memory and compute costs. optimizing compression formats and algorithms is crucial for improving execution time, memory footprint, and energy efficiency.This paper introduces MATANALYZE, a framework designed to generate efficient hardware accelerators for sparse × dense and dense × sparse matrix multiplication. MATANALYZE takes into account the hardware constraints, including the number of DSPs and BRAMs and the characteristics of the input matrices (sparsity and dimensions) and generates optimized custom hardware accelerator designs. The hardware designs explored by MATANALYZE are based on either row-wise or column-wise product algorithms. Compared to conventional methods, these approaches offer more favorable trade-offs between performance and resource utilization. This paper demonstrates that, for a given workload, selecting the appropriate combination of compression format and multiplication algorithm can lead to significant performance improvements, achieving in some cases more than 1000× speedup compared to naive baseline implementations.
title_zh: MATANALYZE - 一种用于稀疏×密集和密集×稀疏矩阵乘法的自定义加速器生成器
abstract_zh: 矩阵乘法是深度学习、科学计算和图分析领域的基础。这些领域中稀疏矩阵的广泛应用为降低内存和计算成本提供了巨大机遇。优化压缩格式与算法对于提升执行时间、减少内存占用以及提高能效至关重要。本文提出了一种名为MATANALYZE的框架，旨在为稀疏×稠密及稠密×稀疏矩阵乘法生成高效的硬件加速器。MATANALYZE在设计过程中综合考虑了硬件约束（如DSP和BRAM的数量）以及输入矩阵的特性（稀疏度和维度），并生成经过优化的定制化硬件加速器设计方案。MATANALYZE所探索的硬件架构基于行级或列级乘法算法，相较于传统方法，这些方案在性能与资源利用率之间提供了更优的权衡。本文证明，针对特定工作负载，选择合适的压缩格式与乘法算法组合，可带来显著的性能提升，某些情况下甚至比原始基线实现快1000倍以上。

title: Personalized NextGen Product Design with GenAI
authors: Adrik Ray
abstract: This paper presents an advanced system design environment (SDE) that implements a data-driven pipeline for product refinement through automated feedback analysis and rapid prototyping. The system introduces a novel integrated approach combining personality-aware feedback analysis, generative AI (GenAI), and 3D visualization technologies in a unified workflow. The core innovation lies in its seven-stage automated pipeline that transforms qualitative user feedback into quantitative design parameters while dynamically adapting to distinct personality traits through calibrated temperature settings - from High Openness individuals (0.8) valuing creative exploration to High Conscientiousness users (0.2) emphasizing structured efficiency. The architecture features a sophisticated Review Analysis Engine employing large language models (LLMs) to process user feedback with personality-specific temperature settings, while the Parameter Generation System converts these insights into design variables. The system's Design Refinement Engine and Visualization System create a continuous improvement cycle where feedback from High Innovator users (0.9) is balanced against High Empathy (0.6) and High Confidence (0.7) personalities to influence physical specifications through AI-mediated analysis and geometric processing. Initial implementations demonstrate significant improvements, with High Innovator users generating 200% more unique design variations and showing 70% higher likelihood of design acceptance. The system achieves 40% faster design iteration cycles while maintaining strong correlation (r > 0.7) between personality traits and design acceptance across all types. This research advances the field by establishing a direct, automated pipeline from personality-influenced user sentiment to physical product specifications, representing a fundamental shift in how diverse user perspectives influence product development while maintaining cost-effectiveness through strategic use of open-source foundations and scalable implementation options.
title_zh: 基于生成式AI的个性化下一代产品设计
abstract_zh:
本文提出了一种先进的系统设计环境（SDE），通过自动化反馈分析与快速原型制作的数据驱动流程，实现产品优化。该系统引入了一种创新的集成方法，将人格感知反馈分析、生成式人工智能（GenAI）以及三维可视化技术融合于统一的工作流中。其核心创新在于一个七阶段的自动化流程，能够将定性用户反馈转化为定量设计参数，并通过校准的温度设置动态适应不同人格特质——例如高开放性个体（温度设为0.8）重视创造性探索，而高尽责性用户（温度设为0.2）则更强调结构化效率。

系统架构包含一个复杂的评论分析引擎，利用大语言模型（LLMs）对用户反馈进行处理，并根据用户人格特征采用特定的温度参数；同时，参数生成系统将这些洞察转化为具体的设计变量。设计优化引擎与可视化系统共同构建了一个持续改进的闭环：来自高创新者用户（温度设为0.9）的反馈，与高共情型（0.6）和高自信型（0.7）人格用户的反馈相平衡，通过AI中介分析与几何处理影响产品的物理规格。

初步实施结果显示显著成效：高创新者用户生成的设计变体数量提升了200%，且设计被接受的可能性高出70%。系统实现了40%的设计迭代速度提升，同时在所有类型用户中，人格特质与设计接受度之间保持了较强的正相关性（r > 0.7）。本研究推动了该领域的进步，建立了一条从受人格影响的用户情感到实体产品规格的直接、自动化的转化路径，标志着产品开发方式的根本转变——在充分吸纳多样化用户视角的同时，通过战略性地使用开源基础框架和可扩展的部署方案，有效保障了成本效益。

title: Large Language Models for Malware Detection: A Systematic Review, Taxonomy, and Open Challenges
authors: Joyce Champie, Karim Elish
abstract: The rapid evolution of malware targeting mobile devices, IoT/edge platforms, and traditional computing systems has intensified the need for detection techniques capable of reasoning about complex code semantics and behavioral patterns. Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently emerged as a promising direction due to their ability to encode contextual relationships that are difficult to model with traditional approaches. This topical review presents a comprehensive synthesis of LLM-driven malware detection across heterogeneous environments. Based on 26 peer-reviewed studies published between 2020 and 2025, the review introduces a unified taxonomy spanning static and behavioral embeddings, prompt-driven analyses, reasoning-enhanced architectures, and security-oriented fine-tuning strategies. A structured comparative assessment across effectiveness, robustness, computational efficiency, explainability, and privacy, reveals consistent methodological trends: fine-tuned and hybrid transformer architectures deliver the strongest empirical performance, while prompt-based and lightweight models offer practical benefits for resource-constrained platforms. The review also highlights persistent gaps, including limited adversarial-robustness evaluation, insufficient attention to privacy and governance, and a lack of standardized metrics for explainability and energy efficiency. The study concludes by outlining key research challenges and future opportunities, including scalable robustness testing, privacy-preserving LLM adaptation, lightweight architectures for constrained devices, and more rigorous human-centered explainability benchmarks. This review aims to consolidate existing knowledge and guide the development of reliable and practical LLM-driven malware detection systems.
title_zh: 大型语言模型在恶意软件检测中的应用：系统性综述、分类体系与开放性挑战
abstract_zh: 针对移动设备、物联网/边缘计算平台以及传统计算系统日益复杂的恶意软件，其快速演变加剧了对能够推理复杂代码语义和行为模式的检测技术的需求。近年来，大型语言模型（LLMs）因其能够捕捉传统方法难以建模的上下文关系，展现出巨大的潜力，成为该领域的一个有前景的发展方向。本文综述全面整合了跨异构环境的LLM驱动恶意软件检测研究进展。基于2020至2025年间发表的26篇同行评审文献，本综述提出了一套统一的分类体系，涵盖静态与行为嵌入、提示驱动分析、增强推理能力的架构设计，以及面向安全性的微调策略。通过对有效性、鲁棒性、计算效率、可解释性及隐私保护等方面的结构化比较评估，揭示出若干一致的方法论趋势：经过微调的混合Transformer架构在实证性能上表现最优，而基于提示的方法和轻量级模型则在资源受限平台中具有实际应用优势。此外，本综述还指出了当前存在的持续性短板，包括对抗鲁棒性评估不足、对隐私与治理关注不够，以及缺乏可解释性和能效方面的标准化度量指标。文章最后总结了关键的研究挑战与未来机遇，包括可扩展的鲁棒性测试、隐私保护的LLM适配、面向受限设备的轻量化架构，以及更严格的以用户为中心的可解释性基准。本综述旨在整合现有知识，推动可靠且实用的LLM驱动恶意软件检测系统的研发。

title: Digital Twins Reimagined: Zero-Day LLM-Powered Moving Target Defense In-Depth for Real-Time CPS
authors: Rajarshi Mukherjee, Mohamed Azab, Thidapat Chantem
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) revolution has profoundly transformed modern industries by embedding connected digital devices across critical sectors such as energy, pharmaceuticals, and manufacturing. Such interconnected devices together form Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), complex structures that enable computation to directly control physical processes. These systems increasingly rely on automation and real-time control to enhance efficiency, productivity, and safety. However, this growing dependence on connectivity has significantly expanded the attack surface, exposing mission-critical infrastructure to sophisticated cyber threats. Among the most dangerous are zero-day vulnerabilities, which are unknown flaws in software or firmware, and zero-day design flaws, which are deeply embedded architectural weaknesses often undetectable by conventional verification methods. Traditional defense mechanisms, largely based on static rules or known threat signatures, are ill-equipped to detect these stealthy and evolving threats - especially in real-time systems, where even minor delays in detection or response can result in catastrophic outcomes. To overcome these limitations, this work presents a transformative security framework that leverages the reasoning and generative capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to construct an intelligent, redundant digital twin of the operational system. Unlike traditional replicas, the LLM-driven digital twin is logically and architecturally isolated, making it inaccessible to attackers targeting the primary system. Crucially, the twin is not a static copy: it is dynamically synthesized and continuously restructured by the LLM to ensure a heterogeneous and evolving codebase. This ensures functional alignment with the physical system while introducing deliberate software diversity, thereby increasing attacker uncertainty and resilience to shared exploits. The central hypothesis of the framework is that semantic deviations between the decisions or behaviors of the digital twin and its physical counterpart can serve as reliable indicators of anomalous activity—whether stemming from external attacks or latent design flaws. By monitoring this divergence, the system facilitates the early detection of zero-day threats and supports real-time mitigation strategies, thereby significantly strengthening the resilience of critical infrastructure. Implementation results demonstrated the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed approach.
title_zh: 数字孪生重塑：基于零日大模型的动态目标防御深度解析，实现实时CPS防护
abstract_zh:
物联网（IoT）革命已深刻重塑现代产业格局，通过在能源、制药和制造等关键领域广泛部署互联的数字设备，推动了技术变革。这些相互连接的设备共同构成了网络物理系统（CPS），即能够使计算直接控制物理过程的复杂结构。随着自动化与实时控制的日益普及，这些系统在提升效率、生产力和安全性方面发挥着越来越重要的作用。然而，对连接性的高度依赖也显著扩大了攻击面，使关键基础设施面临日益复杂的网络威胁。其中最具危害性的包括“零日漏洞”——即软件或固件中尚未被发现的缺陷，以及“零日设计缺陷”——即深植于系统架构中的薄弱环节，通常无法通过传统验证手段察觉。基于静态规则或已知威胁特征的传统防御机制，难以应对这类隐蔽且不断演化的威胁，尤其在实时系统中，哪怕微小的检测或响应延迟都可能引发灾难性后果。

为克服上述局限，本文提出一种创新的安全框架，利用大型语言模型（LLM）的推理与生成能力，构建一个智能且冗余的数字孪生系统，以模拟实际运行环境。与传统的静态复制不同，该由LLM驱动的数字孪生在逻辑与架构上均与主系统隔离，因而无法被针对主系统的攻击者访问。更为关键的是，该孪生并非一成不变：它由LLM动态生成并持续重构，确保代码基具有异构性和演化性。这不仅保证了与物理系统的功能一致性，还主动引入软件多样性，从而增加攻击者的不确定性，提升系统对共用漏洞的抵御能力。

该框架的核心假设是：数字孪生与其物理对应体之间在决策或行为上的语义偏差，可作为异常活动的可靠指示信号——无论其源于外部攻击还是潜在的设计缺陷。通过持续监测这种差异，系统能够实现对零日威胁的早期发现，并支持实时缓解策略，从而显著增强关键基础设施的韧性。

实施结果表明，所提方法在有效性与效率方面均表现出色，具备在现实复杂环境中部署的潜力。

title: AutoTuneGPU: An Energy-Aware Autotuning Framework for Machine Learning Workloads on GPUs
authors: Ananya Krishna Srivastava, Kumari Soumya, Gargi Alavani Prabhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Energy efficiency is an increasing priority for machine learning (ML) developers deploying large-scale workloads on GPUs. However, selecting optimal launch configurations such as grid and block dimensions, threads per block, shared memory allocation, and register usage, remains a manual, performance-centric process that often neglects power consumption. We present AutoTuneGPU, a data-driven autotuning framework that automatically recommends energy-efficient kernel configurations tailored to a given thread count. Our approach first builds a configuration–performance–power dataset by profiling representative CUDA benchmarks that mirror core ML primitives, including matrix multiplication, convolution, reduction, scan, and transpose. We then apply Bayesian Optimization and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) on this dataset to explore the design space efficiently and identify configurations that minimize power usage without sacrificing performance. Once trained, AutoTuneGPU can, given only the number of threads, predict an optimal combination of launch parameters and resource allocations.
title_zh: AutoTuneGPU：一种面向GPU上机器学习工作负载的节能自调优框架
abstract_zh: 能效正日益成为机器学习（ML）开发者在GPU上部署大规模工作负载时的首要关注点。然而，选择最优的启动配置（如网格和线程块尺寸、每块线程数、共享内存分配及寄存器使用等）仍然是一种手动的、以性能为中心的过程，往往忽视了功耗问题。我们提出了AutoTuneGPU——一个数据驱动的自动调优框架，能够根据给定的线程数量，自动推荐节能的内核配置。我们的方法首先通过分析具有代表性的CUDA基准测试来构建配置-性能-功耗数据集，这些基准测试模拟了核心的机器学习算子，包括矩阵乘法、卷积、归约、扫描和转置操作。随后，我们在该数据集上应用贝叶斯优化与粒子群优化（PSO），高效探索设计空间，识别出在不牺牲性能的前提下最小化功耗的配置方案。训练完成后，AutoTuneGPU仅需输入线程数量，即可预测出最优的启动参数组合与资源分配方案。

title: Towards Secure RISC-V Microarchitecture: Vulnerability Classes and Defenses
authors: Mahreen Khan, Maria Mushtaq, Renaud Pacalet, Ludovic Apvrille
abstract: RRISC-V is an open instruction set architecture that enables a wide range of processor implementations across academia and industry. While its architectural simplicity and extensibility support rapid innovation, RISC-V processors exhibit microarchitectural behaviors that give rise to side-channel and transient-execution vulnerabilities similar to those observed in other high-performance CPU architectures. This paper presents a structured taxonomy of microarchitectural vulnerabilities in RISC-V processors, organized into five classes based on the affected hardware components and dominant sources of leakage: cache-based channels, speculative and transient execution, branch prediction structures, memory and address translation subsystems, and observation through performance monitoring mechanisms. For each class, we examine representative vulnerabilities and documented attacks on existing RISC-V implementations and research prototypes, and analyze the underlying microarchitectural mechanisms that enable information leakage. We further study countermeasures applicable to each vulnerability class, covering both RISC-V-specific proposals and adaptations of mechanisms previously developed for x86 and ARM platforms. The surveyed defenses are systematically analyzed in terms of security coverage, performance overhead, and implementation complexity, highlighting both their strengths and practical limitations. The resulting taxonomy and comparative analysis aim to support RISC-V architects, system designers, and software developers in reasoning about microarchitectural risks and mitigation trade-offs during system design.
title_zh: 面向安全的RISC-V微架构：漏洞类别与防御措施
abstract_zh: RISC-V是一种开放的指令集架构，能够支持学术界和工业界广泛多样的处理器实现。尽管其架构简洁且可扩展，有利于快速创新，但RISC-V处理器仍表现出与其它高性能CPU架构类似的微架构层面漏洞，包括侧信道攻击和瞬时执行漏洞。本文提出了一种针对RISC-V处理器微架构漏洞的系统化分类方法，根据受影响的硬件组件及主要泄露源，将其划分为五大类：基于缓存的通道、推测与瞬时执行、分支预测结构、内存与地址转换子系统，以及通过性能监控机制进行的观测。针对每一类别，我们分析了现有RISC-V实现和研究原型中已知的代表性漏洞及其相关攻击案例，并深入探讨了促成信息泄露的底层微架构机制。此外，我们还研究了适用于各类漏洞的防御措施，涵盖RISC-V特有的方案以及对先前为x86和ARM平台开发的机制的适配。所调研的防御手段从安全性覆盖范围、性能开销和实现复杂度等方面进行了系统性分析，突出了各项措施的优势与实际局限性。最终形成的分类体系与对比分析，旨在帮助RISC-V架构师、系统设计者和软件开发者在系统设计过程中，更好地理解微架构风险及其缓解策略之间的权衡。

title: A Distributed Framework for Online Log Parsing with Large Language Model
authors: Shruthi Kandukuri, Prince Kumar, Shubham Pahilwani, Umang Chourey, Sriram Kailasam
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Logs capture information about the runtime behavior of systems and serve as valuable sources of data for performance and security analysis. Logs are often semi-structured; each message contains fixed, constant text written by the developer and variable parts such as parameter values (see Fig. 1 ). Log parsing is employed to transform them into structured log templates for downstream analysis such as anomaly detection, failure diagnosis, etc. Traditional log parsers were based on regular expressions generated by human experts and proved inadequate in dealing with the volume, velocity, and variety of log records. To address these issues, several automated log parsers ( [1] – [3] ) have leveraged Large Language Models (LLM), specifically their in-context learning (ICL) ability, for highly accurate parsing. However, most of them are very slow compared to non-LLM based approaches. EPAS [3] proposed asynchronous scheduling of LLM queries and other quality-related optimizations, thereby significantly improving accuracy and performance compared to other LLM-based works and can be considered state-of-the-art.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的在线日志解析分布式框架
abstract_zh: 日志记录了系统运行时的行为信息，是进行性能和安全分析的重要数据来源。日志通常是半结构化的，每条日志消息包含由开发者编写的固定不变的文本部分以及可变的部分（如参数值等，见图1）。为了便于后续分析（如异常检测、故障诊断等），需要对日志进行解析，将其转换为结构化的日志模板。传统的日志解析方法依赖于人工专家设计的正则表达式，难以应对日志记录在数量、速度和多样性方面的挑战。为解决这些问题，一些自动化的日志解析器（[1]–[3]）利用大型语言模型（LLM）的上下文学习（ICL）能力，实现了高精度的解析。然而，这些方法大多相比非基于LLM的方法速度较慢。EPAS [3] 提出了异步调度LLM查询及其他与质量相关的优化策略，显著提升了准确率和性能，相较其他基于LLM的工作表现更优，可视为当前最先进的方法。

title: Accelerating Parallel Particle-in-Cell Code using DPC++ SYCL on Heterogeneous Architectures
authors: Abhimanyu Karia, Libin Varghese, Bhaskar Chaudhury
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Heterogeneous architectures that combine the strengths of CPUs and GPUs are becoming increasingly important as we move toward exascale computing. Plasma modeling, particularly studying the spatio-temporal evolution of charged particles via Particle-in-Cell (PIC) method, is one of the grand challenges of HPC, as device-scale simulations require tens of thousands of core hours [1] . The two most time consuming modules in a PIC code are 1) linear interpolation of charge density from the particles to the grid (Charge deposition - CD) and 2) interpolation from grid to particle locations to update the position and velocity of particles using the fields present on the grid (Mover). Exascale PIC simulations face challenges, particularly in 1) achieving scalability and 2) ensuring portability across emerging heterogeneous supercomputers [1] – [4] . In this context, we have developed a novel Grid-Cell Map interface that links the two data structures to solve the inherent scalability and portability issues of PIC. Utilizing this Grid-Cell Map approach, we develop a high-performance parallel PIC for heterogeneous supercomputers based on the SYCL framework [5] . We have tested our parallel code on a large shared memory multicore system and the latest GPUs, and achieved a significant reduction in wall time.
title_zh: 使用 DPC++ SYCL 在异构架构上加速并行粒子-网格代码
abstract_zh: 随着我们迈向百亿亿次（exascale）计算时代，结合CPU和GPU优势的异构架构正变得日益重要。等离子体建模，特别是通过粒子-网格（Particle-in-Cell, PIC）方法研究带电粒子的时空演化，是高性能计算（HPC）领域的一大挑战，因为器件尺度的模拟需要数万核时[1]。在PIC代码中，耗时最长的两个模块分别是：1）将粒子上的电荷密度线性插值到网格上（电荷沉积——CD），以及2）将网格上的场值插值回粒子位置，以更新粒子的位置和速度（运动器——Mover）。在百亿亿次PIC模拟中，面临的主要挑战包括：1）实现良好的可扩展性，以及2）确保在新兴异构超级计算机上的可移植性[1]–[4]。在此背景下，我们开发了一种新型的“网格-单元映射”（Grid-Cell Map）接口，用于连接这两种数据结构，从而解决PIC方法固有的可扩展性和可移植性问题。基于这一Grid-Cell Map方法，我们采用SYCL框架[5]，开发了一种适用于异构超级计算机的高性能并行PIC算法。我们在大规模共享内存多核系统及最新的GPU上对所开发的并行代码进行了测试，取得了显著的运行时间缩短效果。

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Hazard Mitigation Strategies in a 5-Stage RISC-V Pipeline: A Benchmark-Driven Analysis Using Fibonacci and Prime Sieve
authors: Kushagra Agrawal, Anindita Basak, Abhishek Raj, Aakarsh Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: This paper presents a quantitative deep dive into the hazard mitigation strategies of a custom five-stage (IF–ID–EX–MEM–WB) pipelined RISC-V processor. The architecture was built with a dedicated hazard detection unit and full data forwarding to effectively handle Read-After-Write (RAW) dependencies and control flow challenges. To evaluate performance, we conducted cycle-accurate simulations using targeted benchmarks: the Fibonacci sequence to stress data forwarding and the Prime Number Sieve to assess branch handling efficiency.The results from rigorous testing showcase the architecture’s robustness. Complete success in hazard mitigation was confirmed by recording zero data stalls across all workloads, demonstrating perfect resolution of RAW dependencies. The processor achieved an efficient Cycles Per Instruction (CPI) of 1.62 on computationally intensive tasks. Even for the branch-heavy Prime Sieve, it maintained a predictable CPI of 2.90. This performance reflects a precise one-to-one correlation between branch instructions and stalls, indicating a deterministic one-cycle branch penalty. These findings validate a sound architectural design and highlight the processor’s suitability for educational platforms and embedded systems where predictable performance is essential.
title_zh: 基于斐波那契数列与素数筛法的基准测试分析：对五级RISC-V流水线中危害缓解策略的定量评估
abstract_zh: 本文对一款定制的五级（IF–ID–EX–MEM–WB）流水线RISC-V处理器中的危险规避策略进行了定量深入分析。该架构通过专用的冒险检测单元和完整的数据前递机制，有效处理了写后读（RAW）依赖关系及控制流挑战。为评估性能，我们使用目标基准程序进行了周期精确的仿真：以斐波那契数列测试数据前递能力，以素数筛法评估分支处理效率。严格的测试结果表明，该架构具有出色的鲁棒性。在所有工作负载中均未记录到任何数据停顿，证实了RAW依赖关系得到了完全解决。在计算密集型任务中，处理器实现了1.62的高效每条指令周期数（CPI）。即使在分支密集的素数筛法测试中，其CPI也保持在可预测的2.90水平。这一表现反映出分支指令与停顿之间存在精确的一一对应关系，表明分支惩罚为确定性的单周期延迟。这些发现验证了该架构设计的合理性，并凸显其在教育平台和嵌入式系统中的适用性，尤其适用于对性能可预测性要求较高的应用场景。

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning on Code Smell Detection
