title: GenAssist: Interactive Prompt-Driven XR Program Generation
authors: Sruti Srinidhi, Akul Singh, Edward Lu, Anthony Rowe
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces (VR)
abstract: This paper introduces GenAssist, a system for generating interactive Extended Reality (XR) programs from natural language prompts. Given plain text descriptions of desired programs, our system uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to retrieve related documentation and example code, which is then used to prompt Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate and execute hot-pluggable XR programs in real time. To ensure that the programs are written correctly to the user’s specifications, we add a closed-loop feedback mechanism using virtual cameras in the scene that iteratively refines the system’s output, mimicking the development cycle of human developers that compile and then interactively test programs. GenAssist generates scripts that can not only place multiple primitives and 3D models in various locations in a virtual scene, but it can also animate and enable user interactions with those objects. We show that across a benchmark of 50 diverse XR program prompts, our system achieves high output accuracy and program generation quality. Furthermore, we conduct a user study with 18 participants that demonstrates GenAssist’s effectiveness and usability (NASA TLX = 36.6) for XR program generation. We compare GenAssist to prior systems and show that it is significantly faster (<10 seconds per run) and requires fewer LLM calls.
title_zh: GenAssist：交互式提示驱动的XR程序生成
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了GenAssist，一个能够从自然语言提示生成交互式扩展现实（XR）程序的系统。给定对期望程序的纯文本描述，该系统利用增强检索生成（RAG）技术检索相关文档和示例代码，并以此作为输入，驱动大型语言模型（LLMs）实时生成并执行可热插拔的XR程序。为确保生成的程序完全符合用户需求，我们引入了一种基于场景中虚拟相机的闭环反馈机制，通过迭代优化系统输出，模拟人类开发者编译后进行交互式测试的开发流程。GenAssist不仅能生成脚本，将多个基本图形和3D模型放置在虚拟场景中的不同位置，还能对这些对象进行动画处理并实现用户交互功能。我们在包含50个多样化XR程序提示的基准测试中验证了该系统的高输出准确率与优秀的程序生成质量。此外，我们对18名参与者进行了用户研究，结果表明GenAssist在XR程序生成方面具有显著的有效性和易用性（NASA TLX得分为36.6）。与以往系统相比，GenAssist不仅运行速度显著更快（每次运行少于10秒），而且所需的LLM调用次数更少。

title: Agentic AI Meets RAG: A Framework for Domain-Specific Knowledge Evaluation
authors: Vijay Kumar K, Abirami A M, Uma K V, Viswanath V S
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Agentic AI framework for QA system development using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The methodology integrates document extraction, contextual chunking, embedding-based retrieval, question generation with semantic deduplication, and multi-agent orchestration. The system leverages the LLaMA 3 70B model deployed on 8 × NVIDIA A100 GPUs, ensuring high fidelity and efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate strong retrieval performance (Precision@5 = 0.91, Recall@5 = 0.88, MRR = 0.87), semantic coverage (0.93), factual grounding (0.88), and a 95% end-to-end success rate in autonomous agentic orchestration. Question uniqueness (82%) and balanced multiple-choice distribution highlight its pedagogical value. The study contributes both applied outcomes—a scalable AI assistant and question bank generator—and theoretical insights into the evolution from RAG to Agentic AI.
title_zh: 代理型人工智能与RAG结合：一种领域特定知识评估框架
abstract_zh: 基于检索增强生成（RAG）的智能代理AI框架，用于问答系统开发。该方法整合了文档提取、上下文分块、基于嵌入的检索、语义去重的问题生成以及多智能体协同调度。系统采用部署在8块NVIDIA A100 GPU上的LLaMA 3 70B模型，确保了高保真度与高效性。实验结果表明，系统在检索性能方面表现优异（Precision@5 = 0.91，Recall@5 = 0.88，MRR = 0.87），具备良好的语义覆盖能力（0.93）、事实准确性（0.88），并在自主智能体编排中实现了95%的端到端成功率。问题独特性达82%，多项选择题分布均衡，凸显其教育应用价值。本研究不仅提供了可扩展的AI助教和题库生成器等实际成果，还为从RAG向智能体AI演进的理论发展提供了重要洞见。

title: LIMO: Large Language Model-Empowered Intent-Driven Network Management and Orchestration
authors: Pu Wang, Tong Li, Chungang Yang, Fuqiang Li, Yao Wang, Qiao Li, Zheng Chang, Bodong Shang, Dusit Niyato
abstract: To support on-demand network services towards 6G networks, we present a large language model-empowered intent-driven network management and orchestration framework named LIMO. Although large language models have already been applied to intent-driven network management, there still lacks an intent full lifecycle design encompassing intent translation, policy generation, code generation, and continuous assurance. To bridge this gap, we present the LIMO framework with intent-enabled, policy generation, and code implementation layers, each integrating distinct large language model functionalities. Meanwhile, we deploy a proof-of-concept implementation of the presented LIMO framework in 6G radio access networks. In terms of the intent translation, fine-tuned large language models achieve 48%-50% higher accuracy compared to their foundation models without domain-specific adaptation. For the policy optimization, the presented framework outperforms five baseline schemes, achieving a maximum service-level agreement satisfaction rate of 96.0%. Finally, the presented LIMO successfully generates executable code for managing and orchestrating the underlying infrastructure. Overall, experimental results validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the presented LIMO framework.
title_zh: LIMO：基于大语言模型的意图驱动网络管理与编排
abstract_zh: 为支持面向6G网络的按需网络服务，我们提出了一种由大语言模型赋能的意图驱动型网络管理与编排框架，命名为LIMO。尽管大语言模型已应用于意图驱动的网络管理，但目前仍缺乏涵盖意图翻译、策略生成、代码生成及持续保障等完整生命周期的系统化设计。为弥补这一空白，我们提出了LIMO框架，该框架包含意图感知、策略生成和代码实现三个层次，每一层均集成了特定的大语言模型功能。同时，我们在6G无线接入网络中部署了LIMO框架的原型验证系统。在意图翻译方面，经过微调的大语言模型相比未进行领域适配的基础模型，准确率提升了48%至50%。在策略优化方面，所提出的框架优于五种基线方案，最高可达到96.0%的服务水平协议满足率。最后，LIMO成功生成了可用于管理与编排底层基础设施的可执行代码。总体而言，实验结果验证了LIMO框架的可行性和有效性。

title: Microelectronics and VLSI Curriculum Development for Potential Workforce
authors: Jabeom Koo
abstract: Rapid evolution of semiconductor technology has resulted in an unprecedented reliance on microelectronics and Very Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) systems across various industries. As technology of this field continues to advance and affects various aspects of the modern world, educational institutions are recognizing the crucial need of a specialized curriculum tailored to these domains. By establishing a curriculum that emphasizes these specialized areas, educational institutions can equip students with skills and knowledge required to address the challenges and opportunities present in the microelectronics and VLSI landscape. Such curriculum shall extend from theoretical fundamentals to practical hands-on experience, exposure to industry-standard tools, and collaborative projects. Integration of microelectronics and VLSI-focused courses will help bridge any gaps between academia and industry standards and will be able to foster potential workforce that meets industry demands. This paper introduces such curriculum for each class year and explores in detail its contents and its outcome. Course survey results from the last 4 years show how students were affected by the proposed curricula.
title_zh: 微电子与超大规模集成电路（VLSI）人才培养课程体系建设
abstract_zh: 半导体技术的迅速发展导致了各行各业对微电子学和超大规模集成电路（VLSI）系统的前所未有的依赖。随着该领域技术的持续进步及其对现代世界各个方面的深远影响，教育机构逐渐认识到，针对这些专业领域制定专门课程的迫切需求。通过建立强调这些特定领域的课程体系，教育机构能够培养学生应对微电子学与VLSI领域中所面临挑战与机遇所需的知识与技能。该课程体系应涵盖从理论基础到实践操作经验，包括对行业标准工具的接触以及协作项目等环节。将聚焦于微电子学与VLSI的课程内容融入教学体系，有助于弥合学术界与产业界之间的差距，并培养出符合产业需求的高素质人才。本文介绍了各年级的具体课程设置，并深入探讨了其课程内容及实施效果。过去四年中的课程调查结果显示，学生在学习过程中受到了所提出课程体系的积极影响。

title: A Cross-Language Approach to Recommending Method Names According to Functional Descriptions
authors: Waseem Akram, Yanjie Jiang, Haris Ali Khan, Furqan Jalil, Hui Liu
abstract: Manually creating concise and meaningful method names is often challenging and time-consuming for software engineers, especially for less experienced and non-native English speakers. They may find it easier to provide functional descriptions in their native languages; however, this practice results in inherent ambiguities (e.g., natural language, semantic gaps, cultural misinterpretations) and limited lexical overlap with English names. To address these challenges, this paper presents LangInsightCraft, a novel context-rich prompt generation algorithm to guide LLMs in suggesting method names from cross-language functional descriptions. From the input description, it retrieves similar high-quality native examples and extracts key contextual elements (e.g., entities, actions, functionality scope) from descriptions of these examples, to overcome inherent ambiguities. It also generates a set of candidate method names for each example’s description, evaluates them against the example’s method name using edit-distance and a semantic similarity index, and ranks them by alignment score. Using these ranked names, LangInsightCraft generates an English textual explanation for each candidate name, which helps LLM handle the challenge of limited lexical overlap between English method names and non-English descriptions. Finally, it embeds these outputs, input and examples into a predefined prompt template to generate a context-rich prompt for LLM to generate the expected method name. Evaluated on 64,815 Java methods with English/Chinese descriptions, LangInsightCraft substantially outperforms RNN-att-Copy, improving exact-match by 54% and 122%, and decreasing edit-distance by 33% and 54%, on English and Chinese datasets respectively, while generalizing well across different LLMs.
title_zh: 根据功能描述进行跨语言方法名推荐的方法
abstract_zh: 为软件工程师手动创建简洁且有意义的方法名通常具有挑战性且耗时，尤其对经验较少或非英语母语者而言。他们可能更倾向于用母语提供功能描述；然而，这种做法会导致固有的歧义（如自然语言模糊性、语义鸿沟、文化误解）以及与英文命名之间的词汇重叠有限。为解决这些问题，本文提出了一种名为 LangInsightCraft 的新型上下文丰富提示生成算法，旨在引导大语言模型（LLM）根据跨语言的功能描述生成合适的方法名。该方法首先从输入描述中检索出相似的高质量母语示例，并从中提取关键上下文元素（如实体、动作、功能范围），以克服固有的歧义问题。接着，针对每个示例的描述生成一组候选方法名，通过编辑距离和语义相似度指数评估这些候选名称与原始方法名的一致性，并按匹配得分进行排序。利用这些排序后的名称，LangInsightCraft 为每个候选名称生成对应的英文解释文本，帮助 LLM 应对英文方法名与非英文描述之间词汇重叠不足的难题。最后，将这些输出结果、原始输入及参考示例整合进一个预定义的提示模板中，生成富含上下文信息的提示，供 LLM 生成期望的方法名。在包含 64,815 个 Java 方法、使用英汉双语描述的数据集上进行评估表明，LangInsightCraft 显著优于 RNN-att-Copy，在英文和中文数据集上分别将精确匹配率提升 54% 和 122%，编辑距离分别降低 33% 和 54%，同时在不同 LLM 上均表现出良好的泛化能力。

title: A one-stop DSL for all your GNN workloads
authors: Anubhab, Rupesh Nasre
publish: 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on High Performance Computing, Data and Analytics Workshop (HiPCW)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are widely used for learning on graph-structured data. Existing general-purpose frameworks such as PyTorch Geometric (PyG) and Deep Graph Library (DGL) simplify GNN development by abstracting hardware-specific details, but their generic implementations often result in a performance bottleneck. The support provided by these frameworks can not be used for the deployment of real-world models. So we present a domain-specific language (DSL) for GNNs, which generates an optimized code for multiple hardware backends such as OpenMP, MPI, and CUDA. The DSL compiler utilizes the same high-level code for generating C++ code for all three backends, even though the parallelization strategy for the backends is different. Our approach achieves substantial speedups over existing frameworks: 7.30-22.76× over PyG and 2.30-43.56× over DGL on CPUs, and 5.44-16.95× over PyG and 2.15-15.64× over DGL on GPUs, across a range of datasets. The work shows that a single high-level specification of GNNs can deliver portable, optimized code for all three widely used backends.
title_zh: 一站式图神经网络DSL，满足您所有的GNN工作负载需求
abstract_zh: 图神经网络（GNNs）被广泛应用于图结构数据的学习。现有的通用框架，如PyTorch Geometric（PyG）和Deep Graph Library（DGL），通过抽象硬件相关细节简化了GNN的开发，但其通用实现常常导致性能瓶颈，难以支持真实世界模型的部署。为此，我们提出了一种面向GNN的领域特定语言（DSL），能够为OpenMP、MPI和CUDA等多种硬件后端生成优化代码。该DSL编译器使用相同的高层代码，为这三个后端分别生成C++代码，尽管各后端的并行策略各不相同。我们的方法在多种数据集上实现了显著的速度提升：在CPU上相比PyG快7.30–22.76倍，相比DGL快2.30–43.56倍；在GPU上相比PyG快5.44–16.95倍，相比DGL快2.15–15.64倍。这项工作表明，单一的GNN高层描述即可为三种广泛应用的后端生成可移植且高度优化的代码。

title: MCPNet++: Interpretable Classification Models via Multi-Level Concept Prototypes
authors: Bor-Shiun Wang, Chien-Yi Wang, Wei-Chen Chiu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Post-hoc and inherently interpretable methods have shown great success in uncovering the inner workings of black-box models, whether by examining them after training or by explicitly designing for interpretability. While these approaches effectively narrow the semantic gap between a model's latent space and human understanding, they typically extract only high-level semantics from the model's final feature map. As a result, they provide a limited perspective on the decision-making process. We argue that explanations lacking insight into both lower- and mid-level semantics cannot be considered fully faithful or genuinely useful. To address this issue, we introduce the Multi-Level Concept Prototypes Classifier (MCPNet), which offers a more holistic interpretation by drawing on information from multiple levels within the model. Rather than relying on predefined concept labels, MCPNet autonomously discovers meaningful concepts from feature maps. To increase versatility, we further propose MCPNet++, which can be seamlessly applied to both CNN and transformer backbones, allowing it to learn meaningful concepts from their respective features. Building on these learned concepts, we also introduce a large language model (LLM)-based method to bridge the gap between these concepts and human perception. Experimental results show that MCPNet++ provides more comprehensive explanations without sacrificing model performance, with the discovered concepts aligning closely with human understanding.
title_zh: MCPNet++：通过多层级概念原型实现可解释的分类模型
abstract_zh: 事後分析和本質可解釋的方法在揭示黑箱模型內部運作機制方面取得了巨大成功，無論是通過訓練後的分析，還是通過明確設計以實現可解釋性。儘管這些方法有效縮小了模型潛在空間與人類理解之間的語義差距，但通常僅能從模型最終的特徵圖中提取高層語義信息。因此，它們對決策過程的視角相對有限。我們認為，若解釋無法深入洞察低層次和中層次語義，則不能被視為真正忠實或具有實際價值。為解決此問題，我們提出多層概念原型分類器（MCPNet），該方法通過利用模型內多個層次的信息，提供更全面的解釋。與依賴預定義概念標籤的方法不同，MCPNet能自主從特徵圖中發現有意義的概念。為了增強通用性，我們進一步提出MCPNet++，其可無縫應用於CNN和變壓器（transformer）主幹網絡，從而能夠從各自特徵中學習到有意義的概念。基於這些學習到的概念，我們還引入了一種基於大語言模型（LLM）的方法，用以彌合這些概念與人類感知之間的差距。實驗結果表明，MCPNet++在不損失模型性能的前提下，提供了更為全面的解釋，且所發現的概念與人類理解高度一致。
